perturbation.original_text: Upon the Company's request, you shall promptly deliver to the Company or destroy all written Evaluation Materials and any other written materials without retaining, in whole or in part, any copies, extracts or other reproductions (whatever the form or storage medium) of such material, and shall certify the destruction of such materials in writing to the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the Company's request, you shall promptly deliver to the Company all written Evaluation Materials and any other written materials without retaining, in whole or in part, any copies, extracts or other reproductions (whatever the form or storage medium) of such material. Certification of destruction is optional and at your discretion.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction. The original text mandates both the return or destruction of materials AND certification of destruction. The modified text makes certification optional. This contradicts the original requirement, leading to uncertainty regarding whether certification is actually required upon the Company's request. The contradiction becomes apparent when considering the 'LEGAL REMEDY' section, which states that breaches of the agreement can lead to specific performance and injunctive relief, making it unclear if failure to provide a certification constitutes a breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: LEGAL REMEDY You understand and agree that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by you or your representatives and that the Company will be entitled to specific performance and injunctive relief as remedies for any such breach. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement by you or your representatives but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original obligation regarding the certification of destruction. The original text mandates both the return/destruction of materials AND certification, while the changed text makes certification optional. This contradicts the original requirement. The Contradicted Text, the 'LEGAL REMEDY' section, states that breaches can lead to specific performance and injunctive relief, making it unclear if a failure to provide certification constitutes a breach if certification is indeed now optional. This creates an in-text contradiction because failure to meet a requirement may or may not be subjected to LEGAL REMEDY.

perturbation.original_text: You will be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by your representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: You will be responsible for any material breach of this Agreement by your representatives, where 'material breach' is defined as a breach that causes direct financial harm to the Company exceeding $1,000,000.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by qualifying the responsibility for breaches. Initially, the agreement states responsibility for ANY breach. The modified text limits this to 'material' breaches causing over $1,000,000 in damages. This contradicts the sections on 'LEGAL REMEDY', as it creates ambiguity regarding the remedies available for non-material breaches or breaches causing less than $1,000,000 in damage.
perturbation.contradicted_text: LEGAL REMEDY You understand and agree that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by you or your representatives and that the Company will be entitled to specific performance and injunctive relief as remedies for any such breach. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement by you or your representatives but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity.
perturbation.justification: The original text states responsibility for ANY breach, while the changed text limits responsibility to 'material' breaches causing over $1,000,000 in damages. The contradicted text, 'LEGAL REMEDY', asserts that money damages are insufficient for ANY breach and the company is entitled to specific performance and injunctive relief for any breach. The changed text directly contradicts the 'LEGAL REMEDY' section, creating an in-text contradiction because the remedies outlined in the 'LEGAL REMEDY' section would no longer apply to non-material breaches or breaches causing less than $1,000,000 in damage. This contradiction is due to the inconsistency regarding obligations, as defined by 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: You also agree not to discuss with or offer to any third party an equity participation in a Possible Transaction or any other form of joint acquisition by you and such third party without CSFB's prior written consent.
perturbation.changed_text: You also agree not to discuss with or offer to any third party an equity participation in a Possible Transaction or any other form of joint acquisition by you and such third party without the Company's prior written consent, unless CSFB explicitly declines to provide consent within 10 business days.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction concerning consent. Initially, ONLY CSFB's consent is required. The modification now suggests that Company consent is required if CSFB doesn't respond within 10 days. This creates uncertainty. The 'NO REPRESENTATION OR WARRANTY' section states that only representations in a definitive sale agreement have legal effect. The amendment in this section weakens the previous requirement, by now considering that silence from CSFB implies consent by the Company, but creates ambiguity with the 'NO REPRESENTATION OR WARRANTY' section, specifically if this new term is not included in the definitive Sale Agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: NO REPRESENTATION OR WARRANTY Although the Company and CSFB have endeavored to included in the Evaluation Materials Information known to them which they believe to be relevant for the purpose of your investigation, you acknowledge and agree that non of the Company, CSFB or any of the Company's other representatives or agents is making any representation or warranty, expressed or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Materials, and riches of the Company, CSFB or any of the Company's other representatives or agents, nor any of their respective officers, directors, employees, representatives, stockholders, owners, affiliates, advisors or agents, will have any liability to you of any other person resulting from the use of Evaluation Materials by you or any other person resulting from the use of Evaluation Materials by you or any of your representatives. Only those representatives or warranties that are made to a purchaser in a definitive sale agreement for the Company ("Sale Agreement") when, as, and if it is executed, and subject to such limitations and restrictions as may be specified in such Sale Agreement, will have any legal effect.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an inconsistency regarding who provides consent. Initially, CSFB had sole authority. The modification states that the Company's consent is required if CSFB doesn't respond in 10 days. The 'NO REPRESENTATION OR WARRANTY' section stipulates that only terms in a definitive sale agreement have legal effect. The amendment weakens the initial requirement, now implying Company consent via CSFB's silence, creating ambiguity with the 'NO REPRESENTATION OR WARRANTY' section if this new term is not within the definitive Sale Agreement. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction', where conflicting obligations are introduced.

perturbation.original_text: STANDSTILL You agree that until two years from the date of this Agreement, you will not without the prior approval of the Board of Directors (I) acquire or make any proposal to acquire any securities or property of the Company, (ii) propose to enter into any merger or business combination involving the Company or purchase a material portion of the assets of the Company, (iii) make or participate in any Of to vote, or seek to advise or influence any person with respect to the voting of any securities of the Company, (iv) form, join or participate in a "group" (within the meaning of Section 13( )( ) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1834) with respect to any voting securities of the Company, (v) otherwise act or seek to control or influence the management Board of Directors or policies of the Company, (vi) disclose any intention, plan or arrangement inconsistent with the foregoing or (vii) take any action which might require the Company to make a public announcement regarding the possibility of a business combination or merger.
perturbation.changed_text: STANDSTILL You agree that until two years from the date of this Agreement, you will not without the prior approval of the Board of Directors, acting reasonably, (I) acquire or make any proposal to acquire any securities or property of the Company, (ii) propose to enter into any merger or business combination involving the Company or purchase a material portion of the assets of the Company, (iii) make or participate in any Of to vote, or seek to advise or influence any person with respect to the voting of any securities of the Company, (iv) form, join or participate in a "group" (within the meaning of Section 13( )( ) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1834) with respect to any voting securities of the Company, (v) otherwise act or seek to control or influence the management Board of Directors or policies of the Company, (vi) disclose any intention, plan or arrangement inconsistent with the foregoing or (vii) take any action which might require the Company to make a public announcement regarding the possibility of a business combination or merger.
perturbation.explanation: The insertion of 'acting reasonably' after 'Board of Directors' approval' introduces an in-text contradiction. While the initial clause requires 'prior approval,' adding 'acting reasonably' implies that the Board's decision can be challenged based on reasonableness. This conflicts with the 'NO REPRESENTATION OR WARRANTY' section, which states that the Company can conduct the sale process as they determine in their sole discretion. Adding the 'acting reasonably' clause could lead to disputes over whether the Board acted reasonably in withholding approval, even if they were operating within their 'sole discretion' as mentioned elsewhere.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You further understand and agree that (I) the Company and CSFB shall be free to conduct the process for the Company's sale as they in their sole discretion shall determine (including, without limitation, negotiating with any of the prospective buyers and entering into a Sale Agreement without prior notice to you or to any other person). (ii) Any procedures relating to such sale may be changed at any time without notice to you or any other person and (iii) you shall not have any claims whatsoever against the Company, CSFB or any of their respective directors, officers, employees, stockholders, owners, affiliates, agents or representatives arising out of or relating to the sale of the Company (either than those as against the parties to a Sale Agreement with you in accordance with the terms thereof).
perturbation.justification: The original text grants the Board of Directors unilateral authority in approval decisions related to the standstill agreement. The changed text adds 'acting reasonably,' which introduces a requirement for the Board's decisions to be justifiable based on reason. This directly contradicts the 'NO REPRESENTATION OR WARRANTY' section (Contradicted Text), which explicitly states that the Company can conduct the sale process in their 'sole discretion,' implying they are not bound by a reasonableness standard. By imposing a reasonableness requirement on the Board's approval, the change creates a potential conflict and limits the Company's 'sole discretion,' thereby establishing a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: You will treat confidentially any information (whether written or oral) that either we or our financial advisor, CREDIT SUISSE FIRST BOSTON CORPORATION ("CSFB"), or our other representatives furnish to you in connection with a Possible Transaction involving the Company, together with analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared by you, or by your representatives (as defined below) which contain or otherwise reflect such information or your review of, or interest in the Company (collectively, the "Evaluation Materials").
perturbation.changed_text: You will treat confidentially any information (whether written or oral) that either we or our financial advisor, CREDIT SUISSE FIRST BOSTON CORPORATION ("CSFB"), or our other representatives furnish to you in connection with a Possible Transaction involving the Company, together with analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared by you, or by your representatives (as defined below) which contain or otherwise reflect such information or your review of, or interest in the Company (collectively, the "Evaluation Materials"), except for information that is generally known within your organization.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an exception to the confidentiality clause, allowing disclosure of 'Evaluation Materials' to anyone within your organization, contradicting the clause's original intent. The 'USE OF EVALUATION MATERIALS' section says the information may only be disclosed to certain representatives. This contradiction significantly weakens the confidentiality protection. There is an in-text contradiction between the broad exception and other restrictions on disclosing Evaluation Materials, creating uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: USE OF EVALUATION MATERIALS You will not use any of the Evaluation Materials for any purpose other than the exclusive purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction. You and your representatives will keep the Evaluation Materials completely confidential; PROVIDED, HOWEVER, that (i) any of such information may only be disclosed to those of your directors, officers, employees, agents, <PAGE> representatives (including attorneys, accountants and financial advisors), lenders and other sources of financing (collectively, "your representatives") who need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction between you and the Company (it being understood that your representatives shall be informed by you of the confidential nature of such information and shall be directed by you, and shall each expressly agree, to treat such information confidentially in accordance with this Agreement) and (ii) any other disclosure of such information may only be made if the Company consents by writing prior to any such disclosure. Without limiting the generosity of the foregoing, in the event that a Possible Transaction is not consummated neither you nor your representatives shall use any of the Evaluation Materials for any purpose.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception to the confidentiality clause, allowing disclosure of 'Evaluation Materials' to anyone within the organization. This directly contradicts the 'USE OF EVALUATION MATERIALS' section, which states that disclosure is restricted to only directors, officers, employees, agents, representatives, lenders and other sources of financing who need to know such information for evaluating a Possible Transaction. The original text made no such exception. The change creates a clear contradiction by allowing broader disclosure than previously permitted, creating uncertainty about which disclosure terms are applicable and enforceable.

perturbation.original_text: OTHER This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties hereto regarding the subject matter hereof. This Agreement may be changed only by a written agreement signed by the parties herein or their authorized representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: OTHER This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties hereto regarding the subject matter hereof. This Agreement may be changed only by a written agreement signed by the parties herein or their authorized representatives, or by email confirmation from both parties' legal counsel.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by adding email confirmation from legal counsel as a valid means of changing the agreement. This directly contradicts the preceding sentence, which specifies that changes can ONLY be made by a 'written agreement signed by the parties'. The contradiction creates uncertainty about which method for amending the agreement is valid and enforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: OTHER This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties hereto regarding the subject matter hereof. This Agreement may be changed only by a written agreement signed by the parties herein or their authorized representatives, or by email confirmation from both parties' legal counsel. This Agreement shall be governed and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York, without regard to the conflicts of law principles thereof. If you are in agreement with the foregoing, please sign and return one copy of this letter, it being understood that all counterpart copies will constitute but one agreement with respect to the subject matter of this letter.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds 'email confirmation from both parties' legal counsel' as a valid means of changing the agreement. This directly contradicts the initial statement in both the original and contradicted text that 'This Agreement may be changed only by a written agreement signed by the parties herein or their authorized representatives'. The added text creates an inconsistency regarding the acceptable methods for amending the agreement, as it introduces a method beyond a signed written agreement, thus creating a clear in-text contradiction as the definitions specify. The change fits the Inconsistencies definition of 'different sections of a contract provid[ing] conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties'.
